root_text.sn22.10:1.4: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako atītasmiṁ rūpasmiṁ anapekkho hoti;
root_text.sn22.10:1.6: paccuppannassa rūpassa nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya paṭipanno hoti.
root_text.sn22.10:1.12: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako atītasmiṁ viññāṇasmiṁ anapekkho hoti;
root_text.sn22.10:1.14: paccuppannassa viññāṇassa nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya paṭipanno hotī”ti.

translation_text.sn22.10:1.2: “Mendicants, form of the past and future is suffering,
translation_text.sn22.10:1.4: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple is not concerned with past form,
translation_text.sn22.10:1.5: doesn’t look forward to enjoying future form,
translation_text.sn22.10:1.6: and they practice for disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding present form.
translation_text.sn22.10:1.10: Consciousness of the past and future is suffering,
translation_text.sn22.10:1.12: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple is not concerned with past consciousness,
translation_text.sn22.10:1.13: doesn’t look forward to enjoying future consciousness,
translation_text.sn22.10:1.14: and they practice for disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding present consciousness.”
